scenarioName: RSS Keyword Alerts from a Persistent Apify Task

scenarioSettings.schedule: Make scenario schedule; do not overlap executions for the same Task

requirements.pollWindowFormula: covered_minutes = pollRunLimit * Apify Schedule interval minutes; if the stored cursor can fall outside this 1000-run page, perform paginated/manual backfill before activation
requirements.runCursorStrategy: single exact-key Data store cursor; do not use Search Records or Data store key-prefix scans
requirements.operationBudgetGuard: Process at most one cursor-selected terminal Task run per Make scenario execution. Repeated scheduled polls catch up a backlog only when makePollsPerHour * maxRunsPerScenarioExecution > apifyRunsPerHour; otherwise pause the Apify Schedule and perform paginated/manual backfill before activation.
requirements.backlogDrainRequirement: Because publication templates process one Task run per Make scenario execution, the Make schedule must poll faster than the Apify Task schedule for normal catch-up; if this formula is not true, pause the Apify Schedule and run a paginated/manual backfill before activation.
requirements.completionBarrier: module 12 Array Aggregator sourceModule=9 wraps the dataset-item iterator route and emits only after module 11 completed delivery writes for the selected run, or emits an empty aggregation when there are zero dataset rows; module 14 cursor is downstream of module 13 outcome and only allowed when completedDeliveryWrites equals attemptedDatasetRows and no module 11 incomplete execution exists
requirements.deliveryFailureStrategy: Module 11 must use Rollback/stop-on-error, not Make Retry/Break. Make Retry saves failed bundles as incomplete executions and lets the scenario continue, which can otherwise advance the run cursor before every dataset row is persisted.

authentication.tokenScope: Reviewed limited Apify token; do not use Make Apify connector or full-access OAuth.
authentication.exportScrub: Remove real Authorization values from every blueprint export and screenshot.

runWindow.sort: desc from Apify with the official terminal-status filter; module 3 filters newer terminal runs after the exact cursor, limits to maxRunsPerScenarioExecution=1, then reverses before module 5 so processing is oldest-to-newest within the selected cursor window
runWindow.officialPagination: Apify task-runs API supports limit and offset; limit maximum is 1000.
runWindow.overflowGuard: Modules 2-4 read the exact cursor record before writes, require first-run cursor priming, and stop before module 5 if the stored cursor run is not present in the fetched page. This prevents silent misses whenever Make cannot prove the cursor boundary.

modules.label: Read Last Processed Run Cursor
modules.route: after module 1 before any product or run-cursor write

configuration.expectedSourceId: last processed Apify Task run ID

modules.purpose: Read the exact-key cursor record whose sourceId stores the last processed run ID. Do not use Data store Search Records or key-prefix filtering for cursor discovery.

modules.label: Preflight Cursor Guard
modules.route: after module 2 before any product or run-cursor write

outputs.cursorExists: module 2 record exists and module 2 data.sourceId is not empty
outputs.cursorFoundInFetchedPage: cursorExists AND any module 1 terminal run id equals module 2 data.sourceId
outputs.reversedRuns: up to maxRunsPerScenarioExecution terminal module 1 data.items[] newer than lastProcessedRunId, reversed oldest-to-newest within the fetched desc page

modules.invariant: This guard uses only the exact-key cursor that existed before this execution writes a new cursor. First activation must prime the cursor or intentionally enable a bounded backfill.

modules.label: Cursor Guard Router
modules.route: after module 3 before any product or run-cursor write
modules.invariant: The cursorPrimingStop and overflowStop routes end with diagnostics and must not reach module 5 or any product/run-cursor write. Prime the cursor or perform paginated/manual backfill before resuming. This stop is required whenever the cursor is absent from the fetched page, not only when the page is full.

modules.label: Current Run Context
modules.purpose: Expose the current cursor-selected terminal run fields for the dataset router and final cursor write.

modules.label: Run Dataset Router
modules.purpose: Route exactly one selected terminal Task run either into the dataset retrieval path or into the missing-dataset cursor path.

modules.label: HTTP Get Dataset Items
modules.capInvariant: This specification is intentionally non-paginated. The saved Task maxItemsPerRun must be no greater than 200 for this product-specific retrieval window.

modules.label: Iterator - Dataset Items
modules.purpose: Split the Apify dataset-items HTTP JSON array into one bundle per dataset record before preparing and writing delivery records.

modules.label: Prepare Delivery Record
modules.inputs: module 9 dataset item bundle plus module 5 current Task run context
modules.validation: validDelivery = itemKey exists AND feedUrl exists AND isNew = true
modules.recordKey: if validDelivery then rss:{{encodeURL(module 9 feedUrl)}}:{{encodeURL(module 9 itemKey)}} else rss:run:{{module 5 id}}:row:{{module 9 bundle order}}

recordFields.product: if validDelivery then rss else rss-diagnostic
recordFields.sourceId: if validDelivery then itemKey else module 5 id + row bundle order
recordFields.sourceUrl: link or feedUrl or Apify Task run URL
recordFields.title: title or feedTitle or Invalid RSS dataset row
recordFields.payloadJson: JSON string of normalizedOutput plus taskRunStatus, defaultDatasetId, validity, and diagnostic fields

outputEnvelope.hasNewItems: completed delivery writes where validDelivery = true > 0
outputEnvelope.newItemCount: count of module 12 Array[] entries where product = rss
outputEnvelope.items: valid RSS records from module 12 Array[]

modules.purpose: Prepare exactly one idempotent Data store delivery record for every dataset row. Valid product rows and invalid/diagnostic rows both continue to the single delivery sink at module 11, so cursor advancement waits for all row persistence.

modules.label: Write Delivery Record
modules.route: after module 10 for every dataset row
modules.destinationKeyProof: rss:<encodedFeedUrl>:<encodedItemKey> for valid rows, or rss:run:<runId>:row:<bundleOrder> for invalid/diagnostic rows
modules.nativeIdempotency: Add/Replace with overwrite enabled leaves exactly one record for the stable module 10 recordKey, including after an ambiguous retry.

modules.label: Aggregate Completed Delivery Writes
modules.route: after module 11 delivery writes, or empty aggregation for zero-row datasets

configuration.targetStructure: completed module 11 delivery write records with module 10 recordKey, product, sourceId, sourceUrl, title, observedAt, validity, diagnostic fields, and task run status

modules.barrierInvariant: This is the Make-native completion barrier for successful delivery writes. Module 13 and the dataset-run cursor can run only after Make has completed every module 11 delivery write emitted by module 9, or after this aggregator emits an empty Array[] for a zero-row dataset. Module 13 must still verify completedDeliveryWrites equals attemptedDatasetRows and that no module 11 incomplete execution exists before module 14 can write the cursor.

modules.label: Dataset Run Outcome
fields: attempted dataset rows = length(module 8 parsed JSON array[])
valid product rows = count(module 12 Array[] where product matches public product)
diagnostic rows = count(module 12 Array[] where product is diagnostic or run-scoped)
cursor write allowed = completed delivery writes equals attempted dataset rows AND module 11 incomplete executions = 0
modules.purpose: Summarize the selected dataset run after the delivery-write barrier. This module is the only dataset path into the run cursor, and it must stop before module 14 unless every attempted dataset row has a completed module 11 delivery write and no module 11 incomplete execution exists.

outputs.module11IncompleteExecutions: 0 required; module 11 must use Rollback/stop-on-error instead of Retry/Break so incomplete bundles cannot continue to cursor advancement

modules.label: Write Dataset Run Cursor
modules.route: after module 13 dataset outcome when module 13 cursorWriteAllowed = true
modules.checkpointInvariant: Write this cursor only after module 13 has completed and verified completedDeliveryWrites equals attemptedDatasetRows with zero module 11 incomplete executions. For dataset runs, module 13 can only execute after module 12 aggregates completed module 11 delivery writes; module 11 must use Rollback/stop-on-error so failed row persistence cannot continue into cursor advancement. For missing-dataset runs, module 15 records that no dataset request was attempted. If cursor writing fails, the previous cursor causes a safe idempotent replay on the next poll.
modules.nativeIdempotency: Add/Replace with overwrite enabled leaves exactly one cursor record for the Task key, including after an ambiguous retry.

modules.label: Missing Dataset Outcome
fields: cursor write allowed = true because no dataset rows can exist
modules.purpose: Report a terminal Task run without defaultDatasetId, record that no dataset request was attempted, and permit a separate cursor write without requesting dataset items.

modules.label: Write Missing-Dataset Run Cursor
modules.checkpointInvariant: Write this cursor only after module 15 has completed. Dataset runs must use module 14, where module 13 verifies completedDeliveryWrites equals attemptedDatasetRows with zero module 11 incomplete executions after module 12 aggregates completed module 11 writes. If cursor writing fails, the previous cursor causes a safe idempotent replay on the next poll.
modules.nativeIdempotency: Add/Replace with overwrite enabled leaves exactly one cursor record for the Task key, including after an ambiguous retry.

terminalFailureRoute.datasetPath: module 13 reports the selected run after module 12 aggregates completed module 11 delivery writes and verifies completedDeliveryWrites equals attemptedDatasetRows with zero module 11 incomplete executions; module 14 writes the dataset run cursor downstream of that real Make barrier only when cursorWriteAllowed=true
terminalFailureRoute.emptyDataset: module 12 emits an empty Array[] and module 13 records zero completed delivery writes before module 14 writes the cursor
terminalFailureRoute.missingDataset: module 15 records that no dataset request was attempted; module 16 writes the missing-dataset run cursor
terminalFailureRoute.then: do not throw an error after successful diagnostics; module 11 delivery failures must Rollback/stop before module 12/module 14 so incomplete executions cannot advance the cursor

errorHandlers.scenario: Set Store incomplete executions to Yes and process in order/no overlap.

destination.resumeAt: next scheduled scenario execution from module 2 exact cursor read; do not resume downstream of module 11
destination.never: Do not use Make Retry/Break on module 11. Retry saves failed bundles as incomplete executions and the scenario run continues, which can allow module 12 and module 14 to advance the cursor before all rows persist.
destination.requiredProof: Inject a module 11 post-commit timeout/failure and prove module 12/module 14 do not run for that execution, the run cursor remains unchanged, and the next poll replays idempotently leaving exactly one record for rss:<encodedFeedUrl>:<encodedItemKey> for valid rows, or rss:run:<runId>:row:<bundleOrder> for invalid/diagnostic rows.
destination.cursorSafety: Rollback/stop-on-error is required for module 11 because it stops the scenario run with no further bundles; successful replay is safe due stable module 10 recordKey overwrite.

preflightCursorRead.resumeAt: module 2 exact-key cursor read before any product or run-cursor write
preflightCursorRead.never: write product rows or run cursor before preflight succeeds

runCursor.resumeAt: module 14 for dataset runs or module 16 for missing-dataset runs
runCursor.requiredProof: Run cursor is written only after module 13 dataset outcome or module 15 missing-dataset outcome; duplicate polls leave exactly one cursor record and do not re-deliver already cursored runs.

publicationGate: Build in a user-owned Make account, configure the scenario schedule, confirm maxItemsPerRun is no greater than 200, and set Store incomplete executions to Yes plus process-in-order/no-overlap behavior.

Use HTTP polling, not the Make Apify connector: list recent terminal Task runs with desc=1&limit=1000&offset=0&status=SUCCEEDED,FAILED,ABORTED,TIMED-OUT, reverse the fetched page before processing, read the exact run cursor, process only one newer terminal run per execution, stop loudly if the cursor is missing or if the fetched page has no cursor boundary, and fetch dataset items with the fixed non-paginated retrieval limit of 200.

Prime the exact Data store cursor before first activation by storing the latest already-accepted terminal run ID in sourceId at the <product>:cursor:<TASK_ID> key, unless an intentional bounded initial backfill is documented. Do not use Data store Search Records or key-prefix scans for cursor discovery.

Keep maxRunsPerScenarioExecution at 1 for publication validation. Confirm makePollsPerHour * maxRunsPerScenarioExecution > apifyRunsPerHour; if not, pause the Apify Schedule and perform paginated/manual backfill before activation.

Use module 10 to prepare every dataset row, module 11 as the only delivery Data store sink with Rollback/stop-on-error rather than Retry/Break, module 12 as the Array Aggregator completion barrier with sourceModule=9 so Make closes the module 9 dataset-item iterator scope only after module 11 writes, module 13 to verify completedDeliveryWrites equals attemptedDatasetRows and zero module 11 incomplete executions, module 14 as the dataset-run cursor write after module 13, and module 16 as the missing-dataset cursor write after module 15.
Prove the reviewed limited Apify token can list Task runs and fetch default dataset items; scrub Authorization headers, Task IDs, data-store IDs, execution data, and sample payloads from exports.

Run non-empty, quiet/empty-dataset, invalid-record, FAILED, ABORTED, TIMED-OUT, missing-dataset-ID, duplicate-poll replay, dataset-retry, run-window, first-run cursor priming, preflight cursor-gap stop, completion-barrier, run-cursor, and Data-store-retry tests; inject a post-commit timeout/failure at module 11 and prove module 12/module 14 do not run in that execution, the cursor remains unchanged, replay leaves exactly one record for the delivery key, and a later successful run leaves exactly one run cursor.
Export and scrub the candidate blueprint.
Import that exact file into a fresh empty validation organization/team or clean account.
Reconnect the limited HTTP credential/header placeholder, save and activate only for validation, then repeat the full matrix after exact-file import.
Obtain independent validator PASS and separate adversarial PASS before publication.
